temporal_description: A close-up, over-the-shoulder shot shows a man with dark hair, wearing a light-colored collared shirt, talking on a flip phone. He is positioned in front of a window with closed horizontal blinds, which create a pattern of light and shadow across the scene. In the foreground, the back of a dark office chair is visible. A watermark with Chinese characters, "风行网," is present in the upper left corner. The camera remains static, focusing on the man's profile as he speaks. A man speaks in a concerned and slightly agitated tone, his voice carrying a sense of worry and frustration. He asks, "怎么闹这么大？" (Why is it such a big deal?). The scene cuts to a medium close-up of an older man with short, graying hair, wearing a black jacket. He is also on a phone call, holding the device to his ear. He has a serious and concerned expression. The background is a dimly lit, opulent interior with a large, out-of-focus golden chandelier visible on the right. The "风行网" watermark remains in the same position. The camera is steady, capturing the man's facial expression. He continues with a more serious and exasperated voice, explaining, "那警察整天跟着我，不是他死就是我亡。" (The police are following me all day. Either he dies, or I die.). The audio is clear, with no background music or significant sound effects, focusing entirely on the man's concerned and tense phone conversation.
